title: Garlicky Pull Malaysian Lacto-Vegetarian Bake
description: A creamy and savory Malaysian-inspired dish featuring a garlicky custard, crispy roti, and a pull-apart cheese center. Perfect for meal prep and a crowd-pleaser.
instructions: Preheat a sous vide water bath to 160°F (71°C).
In a blender or food processor, blend the coconut milk, curry powder, garlic, onion, egg, salt, and black pepper until smooth.
Transfer the mixture to a bowl and stir in the grated cheddar and mozzarella cheese.
Pour the mixture into a 6-cup baking dish or a 6-cup sous vide container.
Cover the dish or container with plastic wrap or aluminum foil and place it in the sous vide water bath.
Cook for 2 hours in the sous vide water bath.
While the custard is cooking, prepare the roti dough. Mix flour, salt, and curry powder in a bowl.
Gradually add water to the flour mixture and knead until a dough forms.
Divide the dough into 6-8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle and place on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Brush the roti with roti oil and cook in a 400°F (200°C) oven for 2-3 minutes or until crispy.
After the custard has cooked for 2 hours, remove it from the sous vide water bath and let it cool slightly.
Place the crispy roti on top of the custard and let it cool completely in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours.
Before serving, reheat the dish in the oven at 350°F (180°C) for 5-7 minutes or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
